earthing_system.code_clause: NEC 2023 Art 250.4(A) — Grounded systems with EGC

met.location: main service panel — exterior wall (grounding busbar)

non_compliance_flags.message: Confirm UFER (concrete-encased electrode) rebar is ≥20 ft continuous and accessible at service before specifying — verify in field.

assumptions: POCO-declared Ze=0.20Ω accepted (typical for utility-grounded service).
UFER electrode assumed accessible from main service panel.

flags: TOOL-CALL-PENDING:calc.zs_loop_impedance — Zs values are LLM-estimates; deterministic refinement deferred per WI3.

rationale.chat_summary: US strip-mall retail fit-out, 200A single-phase split service. NEC grounded system (TN-S equivalent) with main service panel grounding busbar. Grounding electrode system bonds UFER and structural steel per NEC 250.50/250.52. Main bonds at 4 AWG to water and structural steel, 6 AWG to gas. EGCs from Table 250.122 by OCPD: 12 AWG for 20A circuits, 10 AWG for 30A and 60A. No GFCI/AFCI for general retail. Reply to refine, e.g. 'add a kitchen — adds 210.8 GFCI requirements'.

sections.title: Earthing System
sections.summary: NEC grounded system with EGC throughout — IEC equivalent: TN-S. Service grounding is utility responsibility per NEC 250.24; consumer's grounding electrode system is required per 250.50.

decisions.label: TN-S (NEC grounded system)
decisions.summary: Standard US service arrangement with separate EGC.

sections.title: Main Earthing Terminal
sections.summary: Grounding busbar in main service panel on exterior wall. Main bonding jumper inside the panel per NEC 250.28. Main grounding electrode conductor 4 AWG Cu per Table 250.66 for a 200A service.

decisions.label: 4 AWG Cu GEC
decisions.summary: 200A service ungrounded conductor → Table 250.66 row gives 4 AWG.

sections.title: Electrodes
sections.summary: Two grounding electrodes per NEC 250.50/250.52(A): concrete-encased (UFER) and structural steel. Field verification required for UFER continuity.

decisions.label: Two-electrode GES
decisions.summary: UFER plus structural steel bonded together meet 250.50 'all present electrodes' rule.

sections.title: Main Bonding
sections.summary: Three main bonds from grounding busbar to extraneous metallic systems per NEC 250.104.

decisions.label: 4 AWG bond to water service
decisions.summary: Metal water pipe entering building bonded near point of entry.

decisions.label: 6 AWG bond to gas pipe
decisions.rule: NEC 250.104(B) bonding for other metal piping

decisions.label: 4 AWG bond to structural steel
decisions.summary: Exposed structural metal frame bonded to GES.

sections.title: Supplementary Bonding
sections.summary: Not applicable — general retail with no pool, no medical, no agricultural.

sections.title: CPC + Zs Verification
sections.summary: EGCs sized via NEC Table 250.122 by OCPD rating, not phase CSA. The 60 A RTU EGC at 10 AWG is the Table 250.122 minimum despite larger phase conductor — NEC's deliberate decoupling. Effective ground-fault path verified by short circuit length and low Ze.

decisions.label: EGCs from Table 250.122
decisions.summary: Sized by OCPD rating per the table.

decisions.label: Effective ground-fault current path
decisions.summary: Low Ze + short cable runs ensure adequate fault current.

sections.title: RCD Requirements
sections.summary: No GFCI/AFCI required for these general retail circuits. NEC 210.8 GFCI applies to specific locations (bathrooms, kitchens, garages, outdoor); 210.12 AFCI applies to dwelling units, not retail.

sections.title: Compliance
sections.summary: Compliant. One INFO flag for UFER field verification.

sections.title: Assumptions
sections.summary: POCO-declared Ze accepted; UFER electrode assumed accessible.
